quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca, visible from Port Angeles, is home to orcas that can live up to 90 years in the wild. These magnificent whales have unique dialects within their pods, much like regional accents in human language, and grandmothers often lead their families for decades.

short_read.title: How Gardens Help Us Heal
short_read.teaser: Research shows that spending just 20 minutes in a garden can lower stress hormones and lift your mood. Discover the science behind why getting your hands in the soil feels so therapeutic.

medium_read.title: The Elwha River Returns: A Restoration Story
medium_read.teaser: After the removal of two large dams, the Elwha River near Port Angeles has undergone one of the largest ecosystem restorations in U.S. history. Salmon have returned to spawning grounds they hadn't seen in a century, and the river is transforming back to its wild state.

long_read.title: Hungary's Hidden Gardens: The Tulip Heritage
long_read.teaser: Long before the Netherlands became famous for tulips, Hungary cultivated these beloved flowers, brought from the Ottoman Empire in the 1500s. This beautifully illustrated story explores Hungary's gardening traditions and the historic estates where rare varieties still bloom.

port_angeles.title: Black-Tailed Deer at Hurricane Ridge
port_angeles.description: Late May is a wonderful time to spot black-tailed deer and their newborn fawns at Hurricane Ridge. These gentle Olympic Peninsula natives often graze in the meadows near the visitor center in the early morning and evening. The drive up offers spectacular views of the Strait and wildflowers beginning to bloom at higher elevations.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute when active.
puzzle_1.correct_response: That's right, Barbara! Their tiny hearts are incredibly powerful little engines.
puzzle_1.close_response: Very close! The answer is 1,200 beats per minute — absolutely amazing for such a tiny bird!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Those tiny hearts are remarkable — they beat about 1,200 times per minute when the bird is active!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To power their lightning-fast wing beats of up to 80 times per second, hummingbirds have the fastest metabolism of any bird. They must eat about half their body weight in nectar each day just to survive.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful, Ildiko! Pillangó is such a lovely, musical word for such a delicate creature.
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó' — doesn't it sound beautiful? (Virág means flower, madár means bird, and méhecske means little bee.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is part of the Finno-Ugric language family, making it quite unique in Europe. Many Hungarian words for nature are wonderfully poetic and descriptive.

puzzle_3.question: What percentage of the Olympic Peninsula receives over 140 inches of rain per year, making it a temperate rainforest?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Exactly right! About a quarter of the peninsula is lush temperate rainforest, including the famous Hoh Rain Forest where some trees are over 500 years old.
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's about 25% of the Olympic Peninsula that gets rainforest-level rainfall. The Hoh, Quinault, and Queets valleys are home to some of the most magnificent old-growth forests in America!
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The Olympic rainforests are draped in moss and ferns, creating an enchanted landscape. Meanwhile, Port Angeles sits in the rain shadow of the mountains and receives far less rain than just miles away.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
